standard_description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, e.g., interpret 35 = 5 × 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as many as 5. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
subject: Math

Problem 1
Some of the problems below can be solved by multiplying
$$\tfrac{1}{8}\times\tfrac{2}{5}$$
, while others need a different operation. Select the ones that can be solved by multiplying these two numbers. For the remaining, tell what operation is appropriate. In all cases, solve the problem (if possible) and include appropriate units in the answer.
a.   Two-fifths of the students in Anya’s fifth-grade class are girls. One-eighth of the girls wear glasses. What fraction of Anya’s class consists of girls who wear glasses?
b.   A farm is in the shape of a rectangle
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of a mile long and
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of a mile wide. What is the area of the farm?
c.   There is
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of a pizza left. If Jamie eats another
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of the original whole pizza, what fraction of the original pizza is left over?
d.
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of kids in a class are boys. Of those boys,
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
have red hair. What fraction of the class is red-haired boys?
e.   Only
$$\tfrac{1}{20}$$
of the guests at the party wore both red and green. If
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of the guests wore red, what fraction of the guests who wore red also wore green?
f.   Alex was planting a garden. He planted
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of the garden with potatoes and
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of the garden with lettuce. What fraction of the garden is planted with potatoes or lettuce?
g.   At the start of the trip, the gas tank on the car was
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
full. If the trip used
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of the remaining gas, what fraction of a tank of gas is left at the end of the trip?
h.   On Monday,
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
of the students in Mr. Brown’s class were absent from school. The nurse told Mr. Brown that
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of those students who were absent had the flu. What fraction of the absent students had the flu?
i.   Of the children at a daycare,
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
are boys and
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of the boys are under 1 year old. How many boys at the daycare are under 1 year old?
j.   Jason lives
$$\tfrac{2}{5}$$
of a mile from school. Grace lives
$$\tfrac{1}{8}$$
as far from school as Jason does. How far, in miles, does Grace live from school?

items.question_content:
Multiplying $3\times8$ is like having $3$ rows of dots with $8$ dots in each row:

![A rectangular array with 3 rows and 8 dots per row](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc1f80d17a36e3f8143d813354b53ac5290d9c66)

**How many dots are there?**

$3\times8=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ rows with $\purpleC8$ dots in each row.

![The same array with the rows numbered 1 through 3 and the dots in the row numbered 1 through 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68bde365328929dc29aca231997af747ecc4552d)

We can find the total number of dots by adding $\purpleC8$ dots $\blueD3$ times:

![The same array with each row labeled, 8 dots](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/04969d47450b89e256a46f6f60fd24997cbd0018)

$\purpleC8 + \purpleC8 + \purpleC8  = {}?$

![The same array with the dots numbered 1 through 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e503ec97014260d7ec60f52a562efffe36cfa090)

$\purpleC8 + \purpleC8 + \purpleC8  = \tealD{24}$

There are $\tealD{24}$ dots.

$3 \times 8 = \tealD{24}$

khan_exercises.title: Multiply and divide by 10
khan_exercises.description: Practice multiplying and dividing whole numbers by ten.

items.question_content:
$2{,}900 \div 10=$  [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$2{,}900 \times 10=$  [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
When **multiplying** by $10$, we shift each digit $1$ place value to the **left**.

When **dividing** by $10$, we shift each digit $1$ place value to the **right**.

When **dividing** by $10$, we shift each digit $1$ place value to the right.

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$2{,}900$ |$\searrow\goldD{2}$ | $\searrow\purpleD{9}$ | $\searrow\blueD{0}$ | $\searrow\greenD{0}$  
$2{,}900 \div 10$ |$?$ |$?$ |$?$| $?$

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$2{,}900$ |$\searrow\goldD{2}$ | $\searrow\purpleD{9}$ | $\searrow\blueD{0}$ | $\searrow\greenD{\cancel0}$  
$2{,}900 \div 10$ | |$\goldD{2}$ | $\purpleD{9}$ |$\blueD{0}$

When **multiplying** by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the left.

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$2{,}900$| |$\swarrow\goldD{2}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{9}$ | $\swarrow\blueD{0}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$  
$2{,}900 \times 10$ | $?$ | $?$ |$?$ | $?$ | $?$

Number | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$2{,}900$| |$\swarrow\goldD{2}$ | $\swarrow\purpleD{9}$ | $\swarrow\blueD{0}$ | $\swarrow\greenD{0}$  
$2{,}900 \times 10$ | $\goldD{2}$ | $\purpleD{9}$ |$\blueD{0}$ | $\greenD{0}$ | $0$

The answers:

$\begin{align}
&\goldD2{,}\purpleD9\blueD0\greenD0 \div 10=\goldD2\purpleD9\blueD0 \\\\
&\goldD2{,}\purpleD9\blueD0\greenD0\times10=\goldD2\purpleD9{,}\blueD0\greenD00
\end{align}$

khan_exercises.title: 2-step word problems
khan_exercises.description: Solve two-step word problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.  Some questions include estimation.

items.question_content:
I put $75$ jelly beans in a bowl. Each day I eat $6$ jelly beans.
 
**How many jelly beans  are in the bowl after $9$ days?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] jelly beans
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]






I eat $6$ jelly beans each day for $9$ days.

We can multiply to find the number of jelly beans eaten. 

$9\times6=\blueD{54}$

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ jelly beans $-$ jelly beans $\blueD{\text{eaten}}$ $=$ number  left in the $\maroonD{\text{bowl}}$ 



$\greenD{75}-\blueD{54}=\maroonD{21}$
$\maroonD{21}$ jelly beans are in the bowl after $9$ days.

items.question_content:
I used $9$ sheets of toilet paper each day for $7$ days. The roll had $96$ sheets when it was new. 
 
**How many sheets of toilet paper  have not been used?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] sheets
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I used $9$ sheets of toilet paper each day for $7$ days.

We can multiply to find the number of sheets used.
 
I have used $7\times9=\blueD{63}$ sheets.

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ sheets of paper $-$ sheets of paper $\blueD{\text{used}}$ $=$ number of sheets left on the $\maroonD{\text{roll}}$ 



$\greenD{96}-\blueD{63}=\maroonD{33}$

**How much does the cake cost?**    

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]

We can multiply to find the cost for the people at the party. 

The cost for the party guests is $9\times\$10=\$\blueD{90}$.

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ cost of the party $-$ costs of the $\blueD{\text{guests}}$ $=$ cost of the  $\maroonD{\text{cake}}$. 



$\greenD{120}-\blueD{90}=\maroonD{30}$
The cake is $\$\maroonD{30}$.

$\greenD{131}-\blueD{56}=\maroonD{75}$
$\maroonD{75}$ pages are left to read.

items.question_content:
Our skating club is going on a field trip. To cover the cost of the trip we are collecting $\$8$ from each of the $9$ members of the club. We also raised $\$178$ at a car wash. 

**How much money have we raised for our trip all together?**
  
$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:

$9$ members each paid $\$8$.

We can multiply to find the amount collected from members.   

$9\times8=\$\blueD{72}$

Amount from $\blueD{\text{members}}$ $+$ amount from $\maroonD{\text{car wash}}$ $=$ $\greenD{\text{total money raised}}$

$\blueD{72} + \maroonD{178}= \greenD{250}$
We have raised $\$250$ for our trip.

items.question_content:

$\blueD{42}+\maroonD{47}=\greenD{89}$
I have $89$ marbles.

items.question_content:

$\blueD{48} +\maroonD{75} = \greenD{123}$
The total cost of the party was $\$123$.

items.question_content:

I made $9$ bags of jelly beans.

Each portion had $10$ ounces of rice.

$72\div9=8$
Each movie ticket cost $\$8$.

khan_exercises.title: Round whole numbers word problems
khan_exercises.description: Solve word problems by rounding whole numbers.

items.question_content:
A trumpet player spends  $315$ minutes in one month practicing his instrument. 

**Round the practice time to the nearest ten.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints: We want to round to the tens place, so we need to look at the digit in the one place.

The number of minutes was $31\blueD5$.  

The digit in the ones place is $\blueD5$.

Because the digit in the ones place is $5$ or greater, we round the tens place **up** to the nearest ten.

Although $315$ is in the middle of  $310$ and $320$, rounding rules tell us to round **up**.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of minutes rounded to the nearest ten is $320$.

items.question_content:
At a barbecue to celebrate the end of the soccer season, $1{,}354$ hot dogs were served. 

**Round the number of hot dogs to the nearest ten.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] hotdogs
hints: We want to round to the tens place, so we need to look at the digit in the one place.

The number of hot dogs was $1{,}35\blueD4$.  

The digit in the ones place is $\blueD4$.

Because the digit in the ones place is less than $5$, we round the tens place **down ** to the nearest ten.

$1{,}354$ is closer to $1{,}350$ than $1{,}360$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of hot dogs rounded to the nearest ten is $1{,}350$.

items.question_content:
On Tuesday, the school cafeteria served $973$ lunches. 

**Round the number of lunches to the nearest hundred.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] lunches
hints: We want to round to the hundreds place, so we need to look at the digit in the tens place.

The number of lunches was $9\blueD73$.  

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD7$.

Because the digit in the tens place is $5$ or greater, we round the hundreds place **up** to the nearest hundred.

$973$ is closer to $1{,}000$ than $900$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of lunches rounded to the nearest hundred is $1{,}000$.

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD4$.

Because the digit in the tens place is less than $5$, we round the hundreds place **down** to the nearest hundred.

$8{,}643$ is closer to $8{,}600$ than $8{,}700$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The attendance rounded to the nearest hundred is $8{,}600$ people.

Because the digit in the tens place is less than $5$, we round the hundreds place **down** to the nearest hundred.

$6{,}428$ is closer to $6{,}400$ than $6{,}500$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of blocks rounded to the nearest hundred is $6{,}400$.

$4{,}535$ is closer to $4{,}500$ than $4{,}600$.

items.question_content:
The width of a rectangle is $7$ units. The perimeter is $32$ units.

**Which equation correctly relates the given information and the length, $\ell$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's create an image to represent our problem.

The shape is a rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

The width is $\blueD7$ units.


[[☃ image 1]]

The **$\purpleD{\text{perimeter}}$** of the rectangle is $\purpleD{32}$ units.


[[☃ image 1]]

To find perimeter, we add all the side lengths.

$\purpleD{\text{Perimeter}}=\blueD7 + \greenD{\ell} + \blueD7  +\greenD{\ell} = \purpleD{32\text{ units}}$

We can use the following equation to relate the information:

$7  + \ell +7 + \ell =32$

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

To find perimeter, we add all the side lengths.

$\purpleE{\text{Perimeter}}=\blueE{8} + \maroonE{\ell} + \blueE{8} +\maroonE{\ell} = \purpleE{36\text{ units}}$

We can use the following equation to relate the information:

$36 = 8 + \ell + 8 + \ell$

$\purpleD{\text{Perimeter}}=\blueD{w} + \greenD{6} + \blueD{w}  +\greenD{6} = \purpleD{22\text{ units}}$

We can use the following equation to relate the information:

$w  + 6 + w + 6 =22$

items.question_content:
The area of a rectangle is $54$ square meters.  The width is $9$ meters long. 

**Which equation correctly relates the given information and the length, $\ell$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's create an image to represent our problem.

The shape is a rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

The width is $\blueD9$ meters.



[[☃ image 1]]

The **$\goldD{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldD{54}$ square meters.


[[☃ image 1]]

To find area, we multiply the length times the width.

$\goldD{\text{Area}}=\blueD9 \times \greenD{\ell}$

We can use the following equation to relate the information:

$9\times\ell=54$

items.question_content:
The area of a rectangle is $42$ square units.  The length, $\ell$, is $7$ units.

**Which equation correctly relates the given information and the width, $w$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

$7 \times w = 42$
